prompt_text:
### Demographics: 44-year-old Asian woman

### Visit type: Follow-up visit (12-month follow-up)

### Reason for visit / current symptoms
Reason for visit: Routine follow-up (every 3 months) | Diagnosis context: 8 years ago Diagnosed with type 2 diabetes after ophthalmology referral for suspected retinopathy
Current symptoms: Rare nocturnal hypoglycemia (1 episode in 3 months); no DKA/HHS signs

### History
- Other conditions: Chronic kidney disease Stage 2 (60-89)
Heart failure: HFrEF (NYHA Class II)
ASCVD: Ischemic stroke or transient ischemic attack history; Peripheral artery disease
Body mass index category: Normal
Diabetic retinopathy
Diabetic Retinopathy
Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease
Rheumatoid Arthritis
Community-acquired pneumonia (resolved)
- Diabetes-related comorbidities: CKD: Stage 2 (60-89); HF: HFrEF (NYHA Class II); ASCVD: Ischemic stroke or transient ischemic attack history; MASLD: Normal; BMI Category: Normal
- Family history: Type 2 diabetes (father)
- Social history: Current smoker (10 cigarettes/day for 15 years; ~7.5 pack-years); Alcohol 1-2 drinks/week

### Medication information
- Current medications: Metformin 1000mg twice daily; Semaglutide 1mg once weekly; Dapagliflozin 10mg once daily; Glimepiride 2mg once daily; Insulin Detemir 20units once daily; Methotrexate (15 mg weekly), Prednisone (5 mg daily), Fluticasone/salmeterol Inhaler (twice daily), Rosuvastatin (10 mg daily), Lisinopril (10 mg daily)

### Physical exam (current)
Weight 47.9 kg; Height 150.3 cm; BMI 21.2 kg/m²; Blood pressure 138/82 mmHg

### Laboratory values (with date / relative timing)
Format: YYYY-MM-DD value or relative timing (12 months ago / 9 months ago / 6 months ago / 3 months ago / current)
Order: 12 months ago -> 9 months ago -> 6 months ago -> 3 months ago -> current
- Glycemia: HbA1c (12 months ago 11.2%; 9 months ago 11.0%; 6 months ago 11.0%; 3 months ago 10.5%; current 10.1%), FPG (12 months ago 320mg/dL; 9 months ago 320mg/dL; 6 months ago 251mg/dL; 3 months ago 229mg/dL; current 219mg/dL)
- Anthropometrics / blood pressure: Weight (12 months ago 46.7kg; 9 months ago 46.3kg; 6 months ago 45.9kg; 3 months ago 43.9kg; current 47.9kg), Height (12 months ago 150.3cm; 9 months ago 150.3cm; 6 months ago 150.3cm; 3 months ago 150.3cm; current 150.3cm), BMI (12 months ago 21.0kg/m²; 9 months ago 21.0kg/m²; 6 months ago 21.0kg/m²; 3 months ago 20.0kg/m²; current 21.2kg/m²), SBP (12 months ago 138mmHg; 9 months ago 138mmHg; 6 months ago 141mmHg; 3 months ago 135mmHg; current 138mmHg), DBP (12 months ago 80mmHg; 9 months ago 80mmHg; 6 months ago 79mmHg; 3 months ago 79mmHg; current 82mmHg)
- Liver function: ALT (12 months ago 26U/L; 9 months ago 27U/L; 6 months ago 28U/L; 3 months ago 26U/L; current 31U/L), AST (12 months ago 20U/L; 9 months ago 13U/L; 6 months ago 21U/L; 3 months ago 14U/L; current 17U/L), Total Bilirubin (12 months ago 0.4mg/dL; 9 months ago 0.5mg/dL; 6 months ago 0.5mg/dL; 3 months ago 0.6mg/dL; current 0.6mg/dL)
- Kidney: eGFR (12 months ago 65mL/min/1.73m²; 9 months ago 69mL/min/1.73m²; 6 months ago 66mL/min/1.73m²; 3 months ago 67mL/min/1.73m²; current 66mL/min/1.73m²), UACR (12 months ago 4.2mg/g; 9 months ago 5.6mg/g; 6 months ago 4.2mg/g; 3 months ago 4.3mg/g; current 5mg/g)
- Lipids: LDL (12 months ago 75mg/dL; 9 months ago 75mg/dL; 6 months ago 76mg/dL; 3 months ago 75mg/dL; current 74mg/dL); HDL (12 months ago 51mg/dL; 9 months ago 50mg/dL; 6 months ago 51mg/dL; 3 months ago 47mg/dL; current 49mg/dL); Triglycerides (12 months ago 121mg/dL; 9 months ago 123mg/dL; 6 months ago 126mg/dL; 3 months ago 109mg/dL; current 115mg/dL)
- Hemoglobin: Hemoglobin (12 months ago 16.0g/dL; 9 months ago 15.2g/dL; 6 months ago 15.0g/dL; 3 months ago 15.0g/dL; current 15.1g/dL)
- Additional tests: urine ketone Not measured (Current); fasting C-peptide 1.34 ng/mL (Current)

### Individualized treatment decision factors
Current adherence and self-management: Very poor adherence (estimated <50%); SMBG rarely performed; lifestyle control severely limited.
Financial context: Patient has comprehensive insurance with minimal out-of-pocket burden and reports no financial barriers to clinically indicated therapies (budget ≤ KRW 1,000,000/month).
